name: heimia salicifolia (sinicuichi)
mechanism: Mechanism is not yet established with high confidence; reported activity is likely multi-component and may vary by extract chemistry and dose.
contraindications: avoid with alcohol or cns depressants
interactions: Avoid with: Potentiates sedatives and alcohol
Caution with: caution with psychiatric medications [9
safetyNotes: Safe in traditional use, but modern dosage should be conservative, Mild dizziness, lethargy, Low, Not well established, Moderate with responsible use, Dry mouth, sedation, muscle weakness, no established LD50 in humans, Mild, but long-term safety unverified, Lethargy, muscle relaxation, dry mouth, caution, CNS-active effects can occur; avoid combining with alcohol or sedative drugs., altered hearing [9, 4]., Low toxicity, high doses may cause mild sedation or nausea [9

sources.title: PubMed search: Heimia salicifolia
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Traditional Mexican herb used for altered states and memory recall.
dosage: No standardized clinical dose is available; use only cautious, source-documented preparation practices., 1-2 g/day infusion (traditional use)
duration: Duration appears preparation- and dose-dependent in traditional use reports; standardized human trial data are limited.
traditionalUse: Used in Aztec and Mexican rituals for memory recall and divination
